summary.intent: Fix a series of bugs in the Gossamer VS Code extension: server port conflicts on reload, missing database tables on startup, checkpoints not showing in the treeview, and live session updates not working across multiple repos.
summary.outcome: Resolved server orphan detection (kill-then-spawn instead of adopt), fixed pushSchema silently executing 0 statements due to comment-line filtering, migrated from a single 'primary' DB to a per-repo DB registry, and fixed several endpoint/watcher issues for multi-repo support. Server was not running at session end, so live update verification was incomplete.

repo: pushSchema splits schema.sql on ';\n' but every chunk starts with '-- CreateTable', so the old !s.startsWith('--') filter silently discarded all 56 statements — DBs were created but had zero tables.

The server previously used a single 'db' Prisma client bound to the startup --repo-dir; each repo has its own DB with no hierarchy, requiring a dbRegistry (Map<dbPath, PrismaClient>) with allDbs(), dbForRepo(), and resolveDb() helpers.

getDb() in packages/core/src/db.ts caches Prisma clients by path — a corrupt/empty DB (e.g. macOS com.apple.provenance xattr causing disk I/O errors) would cache a broken client and never recover without evictDb().
Git worktrees for checkpoints use 'entire/checkpoints/v1' branch; multiple repos sharing the same branch ref fail with 'already used by worktree' — fixed with --detach flag.
GET /api/repos/current only matched against the server's startup repoDir, so opening the extension in any other workspace always returned null, triggering redundant re-registration.
The shadow indexer writes session events to branches named entire/<short-sha>-<suffix>; the branch IS updated incrementally with each new message, even mid-session.

code.finding: Original pushSchema only ran on the primary DB passed via CLI args; secondary repo DBs in config were never schema-initialized at startup.
GET /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints used bare 'db' (primary client) instead of resolveDb, so sessions in other repos returned empty arrays.
resolveDb probe for /api/sessions/:id only checked shadowSession table, missing sessions that only exist in checkpointSessionMetadata or logEvent.
Added evictDb() export to allow cache eviction when a corrupt DB needs to be deleted and recreated within the same process.
spawnServer() guard only checked in-memory GossamerPanel.server; orphaned server processes from crashed sessions held the port, causing EADDRINUSE on next activation.
fetchSession, fetchLogEvents, fetchCheckpoints never passed ?localPath= — all relied on resolveDb probe fallback across all DBs.

workflow: When a pushSchema-style migration silently succeeds (no error thrown) but tables don't exist, check the SQL parsing/filtering logic before assuming the SQL file or Prisma client is broken.
Testing with 'node --input-type=module' against source files can mask issues in compiled dist output — always test the actual compiled artifact when debugging build-time bugs.
macOS extended attributes (com.apple.provenance) can cause SQLite disk I/O errors; a retry-with-delete pattern is needed for robust DB initialization.

For multi-repo servers, aggregate endpoints (sessions list, checkpoints list, search) must fan out to all DB clients and merge results; session-specific endpoints should accept a localPath hint to avoid probing all DBs.

friction: Three separate root causes were stacked (comment filter, no secondary schema push, corrupt DB xattr) making the 'no tables' bug appear to reoccur after each fix — required deep empirical testing to isolate each layer.
The 'adopt orphaned server' approach was tried first before realizing adopted servers serve stale/wrong repo data, requiring a full revert to kill-then-spawn.
resolveDb probe fallback works but is slow with 3+ DBs and masks misconfiguration — localPath param is optional in the API but should be strongly encouraged.
Server was left running in test state at end of session, causing the 'no updates' complaint — stale process from earlier curl testing.
open_items: Webview API calls (fetchSession, fetchLogEvents, fetchCheckpoints) do not pass ?localPath= — they rely on resolveDb probe which works but is inefficient; the webview should propagate localPath from the session object once it's fetched.

The server still has a conceptual repoDir from serve.ts startup even though the DB hierarchy was removed — serve.ts passes repoDir for watcher/indexer startup but the distinction between 'startup repo' and 'all repos' is still implicit.
No HTTP shutdown endpoint exists on the server — killing orphaned processes requires lsof/SIGTERM from the extension host, which is fragile on non-macOS platforms.
The restart() method in GossamerPanel kills the in-memory process but doesn't wait for port release before spawning — same orphan race condition as the original bug, just less common.

Sessions API and branch detection: currently active sessions show the branch from when they were indexed, not the live checked-out branch — a separate /api/repos/status endpoint exists for live branch but is not surfaced per-session in the UI.
